news.headline: US grants orphan drug designation for Rilzabrutinib in 2 rare diseases with no approved medicines.
news.summary: Rilzabrutinib has received orphan drug designation in the United States for the treatment of two rare diseases that currently have no approved medications. This designation is granted to drugs intended for the treatment, diagnosis, or prevention of diseases or conditions that affect fewer than 200,000 individuals in the country. Orphan drug status provides the drug developers with various incentives and benefits, including tax credits for qualified clinical testing costs, exemptions from certain FDA fees, and seven years of market exclusivity upon regulatory approval. This designation underscores the significant unmet medical needs in the rare disease space that Rilzabrutinib aims to address.

Dow Jones Futures fall as market recovers ahead of imminent Trump tariffs. Market bounced back Monday.
news.summary: Stock market futures saw a notable rebound on Monday, with Dow Jones Futures indicating a bullish recovery, as investors brace for the impact of the upcoming Trump tariffs set to be implemented this week. The positive response in the market comes following a period of decline. The recovery is seen as a hopeful sign amidst uncertainties. The anticipation of the tariffs has prompted caution among investors, with the market eyeing the potential consequences. This development is closely monitored by analysts and experts to gauge the overall market sentiment and potential outcomes.

news.headline: Lucid Group, Inc. prices $1 billion convertible senior notes offering due 2030 in a private offering.
news.summary: Lucid Group, Inc. based in Newark, California, disclosed on April 3, 2025, that they have priced an offering of $1 billion in aggregate principal amount of 5.00% convertible senior notes due 2030. The offering, traded under Nasdaq as LCID, was conducted privately with individuals deemed to be qualified for participation.

news.headline: Counterfeit phones sold with $270,000 stolen crypto via Triada malware infecting Android devices. 2,600 infections worldwide. Stay vigilant.
news.summary: A cybersecurity firm, Kaspersky, revealed the discovery of thousands of counterfeit Android smartphones being sold online, containing preinstalled malware designed to steal sensitive data and cryptocurrencies. The Triada Trojan infected devices are being sold at reduced prices, with attackers having almost complete control over the compromised devices. The attackers have been able to transfer approximately $270,000 in cryptocurrencies to their wallets and are targeting Monero, an untraceable cryptocurrency. The trojan can also steal user account information, intercept texts, and bypass two-factor authentication. Kaspersky researchers have identified 2,600 confirmed infections in various countries, with Russia encountering the most cases in the first quarter of 2025. The malware, which first appeared in 2016, targets financial and messaging applications and can be delivered through malicious downloads and phishing campaigns. To protect against this threat, users are advised to purchase devices only from legitimate distributors and install security solutions immediately after purchase. Other cybersecurity firms have also reported new forms of malware targeting crypto users, including a fake overlay malware tricking Android users into providing their crypto seed phrases and a remote access trojan targeting crypto held in Google Chrome wallet extensions.

news.headline: Leading scholars attended 2025 PKU Shenzhen Forum on AI and Economic Development in China. Held at Wuzhou Guest House, Futian District on March 30.
news.summary: On April 3, 2025, the 2025 PKU Shenzhen Forum took place in Shenzhen, China, organized by Peking University HSBC Business School (PHBS). The event was held on March 30 at the Wuzhou Guest House in Futian District. With the theme "Building Consensus on Reform, Stimulating Innovation," the forum aimed to bring together experts and professionals to discuss important topics.

news.headline: Dogecoin drops 16% in a week, now at mid-$0.1600 after reaching $0.20. Open interest in DOGE futures fell 24%.
news.summary: The popular dog-themed cryptocurrency, Dogecoin (DOGE), experienced a decline following President Donald Trump's tariff announcement, resulting in a drop of over 16% for the week. The cryptocurrency, which had reached a high of $0.1787, fell to the mid-$0.1600 region in response to the new reciprocal tariff structure and the subsequent market downturn. This downfall marks a significant reversal from last week's peak of $0.20, reflecting a loss of over 16%. The price decrease led to a decrease in speculative interest, with Open Interest in DOGE futures dropping by 24% over the week.

news.headline: Trump announced tariffs on all Chinese imports, sparking global market turmoil. China vows retaliation, Asian markets plunge.
news.summary: Global markets were in turmoil after President Donald Trump imposed new tariffs on imports, resulting in China threatening retaliation. Trump's announcement on Wednesday included tariffs on all Chinese imports as part of his trade policy reset. China swiftly responded, denouncing the move as "unilateral bullying" and vowing to take countermeasures to protect its interests. The Chinese Commerce Ministry spokesperson urged the U.S. to cancel the tariffs immediately. As a result, Asian markets plummeted, reflecting fears of a trade war escalation. This development came after Trump declared April 2 as a milestone in American economic independence.

news.headline: Sarah Cuellar appointed as VP of Strategic Partnerships at DataShapes AI, a key player in AI field.
news.summary: A prominent individual previously serving as the Deputy Director of the Energy Portfolio at the Defense Innovation Unit of the Department of Defense has been appointed to a significant position at DataShapes AI. The company, based in Charlottesville, Virginia, specializes in artificial intelligence technologies and distributed AI agents. This personnel change occurred on April 3, 2025, marking a notable development for DataShapes AI.

Rio Tinto invests A$150 million in 100 locally made iron ore rail cars in Western Australia, supporting domestic manufacturing.
news.summary: In Western Australia, Rio Tinto has recently constructed its initial locally produced iron ore rail car, marking a significant development in its utilization of domestic manufacturing within its prominent iron ore sector. To bolster this initiative, the company is allocating approximately A$150 million towards the procurement of 100 rail cars fabricated locally. Additionally, Rio Tinto is continuing to invest in bearing refurbishment for its Pilbara operations. This endeavor is being facilitated through partnerships with Gemco Rail and CRRC Qiqihar Rolling Stock Co. Ltd, with the backing of the Western Australian g.

news.headline: US House committee approves STABLE Act regulating stablecoins with 32-17 vote. Other bills in Congress address oversight and reserve rules.
news.summary: The US House Financial Services Committee approved a Republican-sponsored stablecoin framework bill known as the STABLE Act with a 32-17 vote, sending it to a full House vote. This bill aims to regulate payment stablecoins, a type of cryptocurrency pegged to a fiat currency like the US dollar, requiring issuers to disclose business information and token backing details. Another related bill, the GENIUS Act, is also progressing in Congress, outlining oversight and reserve regulations for stablecoin issuers. Furthermore, a lawmaker has alleged that Trump is interested in introducing his stablecoin to replace the US dollar. The developments in crypto regulation are being closely monitored as they unfold.

news.summary: President Trump has announced that the implementation of tariffs aims to boost investment in American factories. However, analysts warn that as a consequence, car buyers may face having to pay an additional several thousand dollars.

news.headline: Thailand's Maha Songkran World Water Festival 2025 aims to boost tourism, position as top global festival destination.
news.summary: Thailand aims to enhance its standing as a top global festival destination by hosting a UNESCO-listed celebration that will be a focal point of the country's Grand Tourism and Sports Year. The Tourism Authority of Thailand, operating under the Ministry of Tourism and Sports, is gearing up for this prestigious event. Scheduled to take place in Bangkok on April 3, 2025, the celebration is expected to solidify Thailand's position among the top 10 festival destinations worldwide.

news.summary: In Dammam, Saudi Arabia, on April 3, 2025, Nanoprecise Sci Corp revealed that Arabian Fiberglass Insulation Company (AFICO), a Gulf Insulation Group and Zamil Industrial subsidiary, has become among the pioneer manufacturing firms in Saudi Arabia to embrace its technology.

DDoS attacks surge as leading political cyber weapons; 9M attacks in 2024 12.7% than first half; Latin America, Asia Pacific see 30%, 20% rise; 7.9M attacks in H1, 16.8M for year; AI enhances attacks, bypasses CAPTCHA; Role evolving beyond bandwidth to adaptation and persistence. Musk's X platform targeted.
news.summary: In the article, network security firm Netscout reported that global Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks increased by 12.7% in the second half of 2024 compared to the first half, totaling almost 9 million attacks. They highlighted that DDoS attacks have become a dominant geopolitical weapon. The firm noted that there were 7.9 million DDoS attacks in the first half of 2024, with a total of 16.8 million for the year - a 30% increase from 2023. Latin America and the Asia Pacific regions saw significant increases in attacks. DDoS attacks are becoming more powerful, utilizing AI and automation to enhance their capabilities. The attacks are evolving and are no longer solely about raw bandwidth but are adaptive and entrenched in modern cyber and geopolitical conflicts. The article discusses an attack on Elon Musk's social media platform X and how DDoS attacks are being used during critical events like elections and protests. Ashley Stephenson from Corero Network Security highlighted how AI is lowering the barrier to entry for attackers. The article concludes by emphasizing the need for increased cybersecurity measures against evolving DDoS attacks.

news.headline: Trump orders at least 54% tariffs on China, adding to 34% announced Wed & previous import taxes in 2021.
news.summary: On Wednesday, a new tariff of 34 percent was revealed, adding to the two previous rounds of import taxes that have been imposed by the president since January.

news.headline: iQIYI debuts two reality-driven films "Mumu" & "We Girls" ahead of Qingming Festival, China's leading online platform.
news.summary: On April 2, 2025, iQIYI, the top online entertainment platform in China, revealed the release of two reality-based movies called "Mumu" and "We Girls" before the Qingming Festival holiday season in early April. These films are part of the company's initiatives to provide unique content to its audience during this significant period.

news.headline: Founder of OnlyFans & HBAR Foundation team up to acquire TikTok's U.S. operations; bid placed this week. Goal is to maintain American ownership. More details on Benzinga.
news.summary: The founder of OnlyFans, Tim Stokely, has partnered with a cryptocurrency foundation to submit a late-stage bid for the acquisition of TikTok's U.S. operations from ByteDance. The bid was made by Zoop, Stokely's latest venture, and the HBAR Foundation, which oversees the Hedera blockchain network. The official HBAR account posted a cryptic message featuring the TikTok logo and shared media reports about the bid. Zoop has not yet responded to comments. RJ Phillips of Zoop stated, "We are dedicated to ensuring that TikTok's U.S. operations remain in American ownership and control." For the full story, visit a financial news site.

Terra Drone signs MOU with Aramco to innovate drone tech; named world's No.1 Drone Service Provider for 2024.
news.summary: In Tokyo on April 3, 2025, Terra Drone Corporation, the top Drone Service Provider in 2024 globally, announced a significant development. The company has entered into a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Aramco, a major integrated energy and chemicals firm based in Saudi Arabia. This collaboration is set to forge a strategic partnership between the two entities.

news.headline: Pause Technologies finishes Mayo Clinic and ASU MedTech Accelerator; 1 of 10 firms selected globally.
news.summary: On April 2, 2025, in Scottsdale, Arizona, The Pause Technologies revealed the completion of the Mayo Clinic and Arizona State University MedTech Accelerator. This real-time AI platform focuses on supporting menopause. Among numerous applicants worldwide, only ten companies were selected for this accelerator program.

news.headline: Neuralink, founded by Elon Musk, searching globally for quadriplegic patients to trial brain chips allowing control of devices with thoughts. Current clinical trial PRIME study has implanted 3 patients, aiming for around 6 years. First patient reports no side effects after a year, using chip for various tasks. Musk aims to provide "superpowers" beyond computer control.
news.summary: Elon Musk's company Neuralink is seeking quadriplegic patients worldwide to participate in a clinical trial for its brain implant device that allows individuals to control a computer with their thoughts. As of January, three quadriplegic patients have been implanted with the device as part of the PRIME study. The clinical trial will span six years and aims to help those with spinal cord injury or amyotrophic lateral sclerosis operate computers using their thoughts. The first patient, Noland Arbaugh, reported no negative side effects after a year of having the implant and has been using it for various tasks for over 10 hours a day. Musk envisions Neuralink to go beyond computer control and provide individuals with "superpowers." The technology has given Arbaugh hope and purpose, allowing him to find work as a keynote speaker.

news.headline: AI gives voice to stroke survivor after years of silence using brain-computer interface at University of California, enabling real-time speech.
news.summary: Researchers at the University of California successfully utilized a brain-computer interface to convert brain signals into speech instantly. This breakthrough technology marks a significant advancement in communication tools for individuals with speech impairments. The development has captured attention worldwide due to its potential impact on helping those who struggle to communicate verbally. The study involved decoding neural patterns to produce speech at an average rate of 150 words per minute with an accuracy of over 90%. This innovation offers hope for approximately 7.5 million people in the United States alone who experience speech challenges. The research stands as a testament to the continuous progress in neuroscience and its applications in enhancing human capabilities.

news.headline: Ocampo Capital LLC closes Ocampo Capital Fund I LP, investing in 7 early-stage consumer brands including Cohere Commerce, Persephone Biosciences, and Future Fans.
news.summary: Ocampo Capital LLC, a venture firm based in Pacific Palisades, California, has successfully closed Ocampo Capital Fund I LP. Founded by consumer industry veteran Karl Bracken, the fund is dedicated to supporting early-stage consumer brands and innovative companies that are influencing the future of commerce. To date, Ocampo Capital Fund I has made seven investments in companies such as Cohere Commerce, Persephone Biosciences, Semaine Health, Small Wonder, Zeya, Future Fans, and Edu.

news.headline: Coindesk reports on CONSENSUS 2025 (Part 3): Emphasizing real-world utility and user experience to achieve mainstream adoption.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency needs to prioritize practical usefulness and user-friendly experiences to attract wider adoption and restore credibility. This shift in approach is crucial for the industry, especially as the market capitalization of digital assets has reached over $2 trillion. Emphasizing real-world applications and enhancing user experience will be key in re-establishing trust among the general population.

news.headline: Blockchain and AI collaboration at Coindesk CONSENSUS 2025 drives real-time data integration and predictions. Learn more.
news.summary: The merging of artificial intelligence and blockchain technology has facilitated decentralized data ownership and seamless real-time integration, ultimately enhancing predictive capabilities. This advancement opens up new opportunities for more accurate and efficient decision-making processes.

news.headline: COINDESK CONSENSUS 2025: Crypto sees surge in institutional interest, regulatory clarity & tokenization reshape industry.
news.summary: Interest from institutional investors in the cryptocurrency market is on the rise, with a significant increase in participation. This trend is driven by a growing sense of regulatory clarity and an increasing prevalence of tokenization. The number of institutions and companies getting involved in cryptocurrencies has reached a notable level, indicating a substantial shift in the market landscape. With more companies embracing digital assets and the regulatory environment becoming clearer, the cryptocurrency market is experiencing significant transformations.
